channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: CRDTs: How Distributed Systems Agree Without Asking Permission
item.description: Introduction Picture two doctors updating the same patient record at the same time - one in São Paulo, the other in London. Both are offline. When connectivity returns, whose changes prevail? This...

item.description: Bonus chapter — Engineering Intent Series Live demo: dungeon-demo-isl.netlify.app Source: example/dungeon/ in the ISL repository The first four articles of this series argued, from different...

item.title: KiroHub: Generate a Kiro Skill in 60 Seconds Built With Bedrock Registry and AgentCore Harness
item.description: I used two Amazon Bedrock AgentCore capabilities, Amazon Bedrock Registry for hybrid search over 10k+ Kiro resources, and AgentCore Harness for testing generated skills against a real agent, to...

item.title: We forked Apache Stateful Functions for Flink 2.x — here's why
item.description: Apache Stateful Functions is one of the quietly powerful frameworks in the Flink ecosystem - durable per-key state, exactly-once messaging, polyglot remote functions, all on top of Apache Flink....

item.title: From Rules to Palettes: Using Color as a Syntax for Cellular Automata
item.description: Motivation: Color as a “Type System” for Pictures If you have ever looked at a black-and-white cellular automaton that felt like pure static, you are not alone. Many classical diagrams of...

item.title: The Dangerous Bugs Are the Ones That Don't Crash — Building Input Validation for My MCP Server
item.description: I was building an MCP server for an event platform that automates speaker communications (confirmations, reminders, calendar invites, follow-ups). An agent created a session confirmation for...

item.title: Three Different Ways to Access Oracle AI Agent — And Why It Matters
item.description: Oracle AI Agent Studio brings intelligent, role-aware agents directly into your workflow. But did you know there's more than one way to get there? Let's break down the three access methods and...

item.title: Built 40+ Free LinkedIn Tools as a Side Project (React + Vercel)
item.description: Hey dev community! 👋 I built LinkedForge — a collection of 40+ free tools for LinkedIn. No signup, no paywall, no data collection. What It Does CV Generator (10+ templates, PDF export) Post...

item.title: 5 Ways Firefox Extension New Tab Pages Are Killing Your Browser Performance
item.description: The Hidden Performance Cost of New Tab Extensions You install a new tab extension to make your browser nicer. You get weather, a clock, a background image. But then you notice Firefox feels......

item.title: TestSprite: The AI Testing Agent I Wish I Had Years Ago
item.description: tl;dr: TestSprite automates end-to-end testing via AI agents in 10-20 minutes. Zero manual QA work. Solid product—but there are locale/timezone gotchas if you're testing internationally. Here's...

item.title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art
item.description: The ad comes from Artisan, the AI startup behind billboards urging businesses to "stop hiring humans."

item.title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
item.description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

item.title: Reggie Fils-Aimé says Amazon once asked Nintendo to break the law
item.description: Way back in the DS days, Nintendo decided to stop selling to Amazon. During a recent lecture at NYU, former Nintendo of America President Reggie Fils-Aimé said it was because Amazon was seeking...

item.title: Shokz’s bassy OpenRun Pro 2 are $40 off thanks to a new Mother’s Day promo
item.description: If you’re looking to pick up a pair of open-ear headphones for yourself — or your mom — Shokz is running a Mother’s Day sale. Now through May 10th, the company’s best pair of bone conduction...

item.title: These reusable digital Polaroids are a clever way to cover a fridge in memories
item.description: I love when my friends have their fridges covered in Polaroids. It’s often a visual crash course of what’s been going on in their lives chronicling recent adventures, get-togethers, and other fun...

item.title: How the internet’s favorite squirrel dad made the hottest camera app of 2026
item.description: It's not hyperbole to call DualShot Recorder an overnight sensation. It took only 12 hours from the time it was released to hit number one on the App Store's list of top paid apps. It was a...

item.title: AI music is flooding streaming services — but who wants it?
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on how AI is changing music and the music industry, follow Terrence O'Brien. The Stepback...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited Linux Root Access Bug CVE-2026-31431 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting various Linux distributions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...

item.title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
item.description: Bad news for Tilly Norwood.

item.title: Meta’s historic loss in court could cost a lot more than $375 million
item.description: New Mexico Attorney General Raúl Torrez won a historic sum of $375 million in a landmark child safety case against Meta earlier this year. But the next stage of the fight could be even more...

item.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after Trump’s war on Iran doubled jet fuel prices
item.description: The ultra-low-cost air carrier Spirit Airlines shut down operations after 34 years in business and canceled all flights at 3AM ET on Saturday morning. Its website now redirects to...

item.title: Aura’s delightful Aspen photo frame is on sale for $30 off this weekend
item.description: Aura’s digital frames are kind of like living photo albums that get better with time, which is why we often recommend them for Mother’s Day. They’re gifts that keep on giving, in a way, and right...

item.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
item.description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

item.title: Anker’s discounted 2-in-1 USB-C cable is a great way to spend $15
item.description: I’ll never stop gushing about 2-in-1 USB-C cables. They’re really nice to have because, at this point, I’ve amassed so many devices that charge via USB-C. It’s also common for more than one to...

item.title: Playing Esoteric Ebb is like rolling the dice with a great DM
item.description: It took me a while to get into Esoteric Ebb, a new CRPG from developer Christoffer Bodegård. The elevator pitch is basically Disco Elysium, but in the fantasy style of Dungeons & Dragons: You play...

item.title: Disneyland Now Uses Face Recognition on Visitors
item.description: Plus: The NSA tests Anthropic’s Mythos Preview to find vulnerabilities, a Finnish teen is charged over the Scattered Spider hacking spree, and more.

item.title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
item.description: Cybersecurity company Trellix has announced that it suffered a breach that enabled unauthorized access to a "portion" of its source code. It said it "recently identified" the compromise of its...

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now:...

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
item.description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had...
